title: Fix webui module rendering (invisible ModuleNode)
description:
**Problem:** Module nodes are invisible on the graph canvas. System and Container nodes render correctly.

**Root cause:** ModuleNode unconditionally applies the 'settling' class (app.js:585), triggering recon-settle animation with animation-fill-mode: both on SVG <g> elements. This strands module nodes at opacity issues.

**Fix:** Drop the unconditional settling class or gate it on real state transitions.

**Acceptance:**
- All 23 nodes visible including modules
- tests/graph_explorer.rs asserts /api/graph returns module nodes
- dogfood + clippy/fmt/test green
close_reason: Closed

title: Epic: orchestrator-agnostic CAIRN with pluggable storage + reference adapters
description: GH #95. Three-layer architecture: semantic core (CAIRN), pluggable storage (filesystem default, optional Beads), pluggable orchestrator adapter (Gas City reference + future adapters). Tracks sub-issues covering integration contract, StateBackend trait, stable JSON, Beads backend, Gas City pack, OpenSpec retirement.
close_reason: Closed

title: Implement spec.md:318 integrity rule: warn on leaf node with no contract
description: spec.md:318 states: 'Every leaf node should have a contract. Missing contracts are warnings, except when a node transitions from ghost to synced (then required).' This is SPECIFIED but UNIMPLEMENTED. validate_contracts (src/map/build.rs:189-207) only flags CAIRN_CONTRACT_MISSING when a node DECLARES a contract pointer whose file is absent; it never flags a leaf that declares NO contract. Evidence: cairn's own 24 nodes have 0 contracts yet cairn scan is clean, so cairn is blind to the exact drift it exists to catch, on itself. Fix: add a check that, for each leaf node (no children) that is synced/owns code and declares no contract pointer, emits a Warning (Error on ghost->synced transition, per spec). New finding code (e.g. CAIRN_CONTRACT_LEAF_UNCOVERED) registered in docs/registries/error-codes.md. Likely loose-by-default / strict-mode to avoid hostile brownfield onboarding (mirror the test-coverage gate's loose/strict pattern). AC: a leaf with no contract produces a Warning under the rule; cairn's own repo then surfaces its 24 uncovered leaves (separate bead to author the contracts).
close_reason: Implemented CAIRN_CONTRACT_LEAF_UNCOVERED (CK003); follow-up cairn-y5f tracks authoring the 22 contracts

title: cairn context omits the dependency graph (edges) it exists to surface
description: cairn context is named 'Structured project overview for agents' but emits only edge_count (a number), never the edges themselves, in BOTH human and --json output. Human output is a flat node list (id, name, status, path) where path duplicates the file tree. Measured on cairn's own repo (24 nodes, 27 edges, prefixes stripped): current list ~371 tokens conveying 0 edges; the full 27 LABELED edges as a flat adjacency block ~236 tokens (cheaper than today AND carries the graph). Fix: (1) human context includes a labeled adjacency block (e.g. 'scanner -> blueprint  # Parses blueprint files'); (2) --json emits a real edges[] array (source,target,label), not just edge_count. AC: cairn context and cairn context --json both expose all blueprint edges with labels; token cost not higher than today.
close_reason: context exposes labeled edges in human + --json (dec.context-edges)

title: cairn init: detect already-initialized project, backfill instead of re-announcing fresh scaffold
close_reason: Closed

title: No safe one-step agent orientation: cairn can't hand a fresh agent {next task + binding constraints} as one un-skippable brief
description: The core value prop is: a fresh agent picks up the next task and the repo constrains it from doing the wrong thing, without the human babysitting. Verified 2026-06-26 that this only half-holds. Telling an agent 'work the next cairn task' is NOT safe today; you must spell out: build release, read AGENTS.md + docs/agent/cairn-dev-workflow.md, run bd ready, work the top item, run cairn rationale on touched nodes first. Three gaps: (1) cairn's own next-work surface is BLIND to the bead backlog: cairn status/changes report 'Active changes: None / Open todos: None' while bd ready lists 3 P-items (cairn-e12 etc.); status and bd are disjoint surfaces the agent must know to reconcile. (2) Constraint-loading is PULL-ONLY and unenforced: the loop says 'if cairn rationale surfaces a decision, respect it' but nothing forces querying the right node, and key binding rules live in spec/docs not node-linked decisions (e.g. the canonical changes-delta format in docs/changes.md that cairn-e12 must honor is not returned by cairn rationale cairn.brownfield). (3) No cairn next / cairn brief <id> that emits {task + governing decisions + contract + acceptance + the gates that will judge it} as one bundle. The gates (scan/dogfood/cargo) mechanically protect code-vs-contract drift and build breakage (that half of 'dont worry' is real) but NOT task-selection or code-vs-decision-intent.
acceptance_criteria: A single deterministic entrypoint (e.g. cairn next or cairn brief <id>) returns the next ready unit fused with its binding decisions, contract, acceptance criteria, and the gates that will judge it. cairn's what's-next surface reflects the bead backlog (or explicitly delegates to bd ready). Verified by a dry run: a fresh agent given only 'work the next cairn task' lands on the correct unit with its constraints loaded, and a deliberately decision-violating attempt is caught or flagged.
notes: Scope note (2026-06-26, verified against the binary): 'cairn next' ALREADY exists and surfaces the top bead (or 'nothing to do. Project is clean.'), so the orientation half is partly shipped. Two gaps remain: (1) it reads the COMMITTED .beads/issues.jsonl export, not live Dolt, so it can lag bd ready (the staleness that forces the two-reader cross-check in cairn-loop-terminal-state-stop) -> make it authoritative on live state; (2) it returns only the task line, not the fused {binding decisions + contract + acceptance + gates} brief. Minor: 'cairn next' is missing from 'cairn --help'. When (1)+(2) land, the prose terminal-stop skill collapses to 'run cairn next, trust it.'
close_reason: Closed

title: Brownfield init --from-code discover->apply round-trip is broken: blueprint never gains nodes
description: Verified 2026-06-26 by running target/release/cairn init --from-code on BurntSushi/ripgrep cloned to /tmp. Discovery is solid (deterministic FS walk, 14 modules found, offline ~3s) but the delta never applies. THREE confirmed causes: (1) FORMAT MISMATCH: emitter src/brownfield/mod.rs:86-105 writes '# Blueprint delta' + '+ Module "name" id "id" {...}', but parser src/changes/delta.rs:33-48 only reads canonical '## ADDED Nodes/## ADDED Edges' with un-prefixed 'Module X "x" id "app.x" {...}' (docs/changes.md:26-51). (2) NO BASE BLUEPRINT: init --from-code writes no cairn.blueprint, so scan/changes/archive exit 1 'failed to read blueprint'. (3) MISSING FRONTMATTER: contract stubs lack 'operation: added' (deferred P-05, declared-items.md:85). Empirical no-op: after patching frontmatter, archive leaves cairn.blueprint UNCHANGED (zero nodes added). This is cairn's flagship 'ingest an existing codebase' promise and it does not work end-to-end on an external repo.
acceptance_criteria: Round-trip test exists: discover on an external repo -> archive -> assert cairn.blueprint gained the discovered nodes. Emitter produces canonical '## ADDED Nodes/Edges' the parser accepts. init --from-code seeds/merges a base blueprint (or greenfield-first is documented + enforced). Contract stubs carry 'operation: added' frontmatter (closes P-05). proposal.md Candidates section is populated.
close_reason: Already fixed on main (f16dae8). Verified empirically 2026-06-26: cairn init --from-code on a synthetic external repo seeds a base cairn.blueprint, scan is clean, and `cairn archive brownfield-init` reports "3 added_nodes, 6 added_edges" and the discovered Module nodes land in cairn.blueprint. All three causes from the report are resolved: (1) emitter src/brownfield/mod.rs:blueprint_delta now writes canonical ## ADDED Nodes/## ADDED Edges that src/changes/delta.rs parses; (2) init --from-code seeds a comment-only base blueprint; (3) post-delta validation + operation:added frontmatter. Governed by accepted dec.brownfield-init-round-trip and regression-guarded by tests/phase_9_brownfield.rs::test_init__round_trip_archive_merges_nodes_into_blueprint (asserts blueprint gains src.alpha/src.beta). Note: cairn refine shares the defect via its own emitter, tracked separately as cairn-h8o.

title: Next phase: webui repair + MAS orchestration hardening
description:
**Epic tracking the next development phase for cairn.**

**Context:** PR #127 merged MAS orchestration foundations (health, remediate, dogfood, scan caching). All quality gates pass and cairn health reports clean.

**Priorities:**
- P0: cairn-s9r — Fix webui module rendering (invisible ModuleNode)
- P1: cairn-42m — MAS orchestration: human renderers + node-specific remediate + cairn next driver
- P2: cairn-95q — Webui server hardening: cache invalidation, timeouts, CI smoke tests

**Exit criteria:**

- cairn health / remediate / next work without --json

title: MAS orchestration: human renderers + node-specific remediate + cairn next driver
description:
**Problem:** health and remediate error without --json, remediate emits generic placeholders, no loop-driver command exists, ~10 finding codes unmapped.

**Gaps:**
- cairn health / remediate require --json (no human renderer)
- remediate emits generic cairn summarise <node-id> instead of real node IDs
- No cairn next driver to print single next action + clean flag
- ~10 finding codes unmapped (CAIRN_ORDER_CYCLE, CH001, CAIRN_PATH_GITIGNORED, CAIRN_CONTRACT_WRONG_NODE...)

**Fix:**
- Add human renderers for health/remediate
- Make remediate emit node-specific actions using Finding.node
- Add cairn next command printing single next action + clean flag
- Map all unmapped finding codes to actions

**Acceptance:**
- cairn health / remediate / next work without --json
- remediate emits real node IDs
- every emitted finding code maps to an action
- following actions provably reaches clean=true
- dogfood gate asserts clean

title: Primitive for Designed-but-unimplemented rules/capabilities (a 'ghost task'), per spec:24
description: Cairn has ghost NODES (NodeState::Ghost, spec:612 - declared structure, no code yet) and #[cairn_planned(phase=N)] for planned TESTS, but NO primitive for a Designed-but-unimplemented RULE/BEHAVIOUR/CAPABILITY. spec.md:24 mandates that implementation status live 'in the project's own Cairn state, not in the spec itself', yet there is no mechanism that turns a Designed spec rule into tracked cairn state. Consequence: integrity rules like leaf-contract (spec:318) rot in prose and slip (cairn-481). Decide the shape: options (a) convention only - every Designed spec rule gets a tracking bead labeled spec:<section> (cheapest, no code); (b) a lint that cross-references a machine-readable spec-rule registry against emitted finding codes and warns on Designed-but-unemitted rules (dogfoodable, deterministic); (c) a first-class 'planned rule / ghost task' artefact analogous to ghost nodes. Recommend (a)+(b): a registry of Designed rules + a scan check that flags any with no implementation, mirroring how cairn already gates declared-vs-real for nodes. AC: a decision artefact (dec.*) recording the chosen mechanism, linked to this analysis. Depends on the audit.
notes: Recommendation drafted and persisted: meta/decisions/dec.ghost-rule-tracking.md (status: proposed), recommending option B (spec-rule registry + scan check). Surfaced on a timed escalation with no live ratification, so per the dev-loop unattended protocol the recommendation is persisted and this bead is deferred pending maintainer ratification rather than self-executed. On ratification: flip the decision to accepted and open an implementation bead for option B.

title: Audit spec.md Designed items vs implementation; mint tracking beads for gaps
description: spec.md:22-24 defines Declared->Designed->Implemented, and says implementation status is 'tracked in the project's own Cairn state, not in the spec itself.' That tracking is not enforced, so Designed-but-unbuilt items rot in prose (proven by cairn-481, the leaf-contract integrity rule that was Designed at spec:318 but never implemented and silently passed scan). Do a one-time exhaustive pass: enumerate every Designed integrity rule, freshness rule, and v1 capability in spec.md; check each against emitted finding codes / scanner checks / closed beads; for each confirmed gap, file a tracking bead. Known status so far: all artefact-link integrity rules implemented EXCEPT leaf-contract (cairn-481); edge drift closed via cairn-9v1; CANDIDATE GAP to confirm = docstring drift detection (spec phase 5: docstring vs map facts -> rationale tension; cairn docstring generator exists but no drift finding appears to be emitted; registry CE009 has no emission); decision-violation gating (spec:71) is explicitly v2-deferred, not a miss. AC: a written gap list (spec ref -> implemented? -> bead id), and a bead per confirmed unimplemented Designed item.
close_reason: Audit complete. Gap list: meta/research/res.spec-designed-audit.md. Every Designed integrity/freshness rule and all but two rationale tensions are implemented. 2 confirmed gaps filed: cairn-ay5 (research-orphan tension, spec:632) and the revisit-trigger relevance tension (spec:634). Edge-validation (CE001-003) and docstring-drift (CE004-009) are Declared (sec 17), not Designed gaps; decision-violation gating is v2-deferred; todo coverage is informational.

title: No CLI surface for node-linked beads: per-node backlog view is webui-only
description: The node-linked beads view (backlog::for_node, src/state/backlog.rs) has exactly ONE caller: src/ui/api.rs:175 (webui inspector). No CLI command exposes it. 'cairn todos <node>' reads markdown Todo ARTEFACTS (artefacts handler), not beads, so it returns None even when beads carry a correct cairn-node:<id> label. Consequence: an agent/human at the terminal cannot see a node's linked beads at all; the cairn-node linkage convention pays off only in the webui. This compounds the 0/128 adoption of the label convention (decided in dec.beads-task-layer, view shipped in cairn-dqx). Fix: add a CLI command (e.g. 'cairn backlog <node>' or fold beads into 'cairn neighbourhood <node>') that renders backlog::for_node output, mirroring the webui inspector. AC: a bead labeled cairn-node:cairn.kernel.query is visible from the CLI for that node. Related: surface the cairn-node convention in AGENTS.md (separate concern).
close_reason: added 'cairn backlog <node>' CLI command (human + --json), mirrors webui inspector

title: cairn context --mermaid: human-facing diagram projection of the rolled-up graph
description: Add a --mermaid (or --format mermaid) flag rendering the container-level rolled-up graph as mermaid for HUMAN consumption (terminals like OMP render mermaid to ASCII). NOT the agent default: for an LLM consumer, labeled adjacency is ~2x denser than mermaid (mermaid pays graph TD + per-edge --> + |label| + indentation). At container granularity (~10 nodes) a mermaid is readable for humans. AC: cairn context --mermaid emits valid mermaid of the (depth-capped, rolled-up) graph; agent default stays adjacency. Depends on rollup/depth.
close_reason: Closed

title: cairn context: container-level edge rollup + --depth/--scope flags for large repos
description: cairn context has no depth/scope flag (only global --file/--json/--strict), and container nodes carry no aggregated edges: 'cairn neighbourhood cairn.kernel' returns None because edges live on leaf children. So a default full-depth dump does not scale to a 200-module monorepo. Add: (1) edge rollup to the container layer (a leaf-in-X -> leaf-in-Y edge surfaces as an X->Y container edge with merged labels); (2) --depth N (default cap at system+container, ~top 2 levels) collapsing deeper subtrees to counts; (3) --scope <node> for full detail of one subtree; (4) each collapsed subtree prints the drill-down command (cairn neighbourhood <node>). AC: on a deep graph, default context shows a bounded container-level rolled-up labeled graph; --depth/--scope expand on demand. Depends on the edges-in-context bug.
close_reason: Closed

title: cairn refine emits non-canonical blueprint delta the parser can't read (same bug class as cairn-e12)
description: src/brownfield/refine.rs:172 blueprint_delta_with_renames emits the OLD format ('# Blueprint delta' header, '+ Module ...' nodes, nested 'edge -> ...') that src/changes/delta.rs cannot parse, so 'cairn refine' -> 'cairn archive' never updates the blueprint. cairn-e12 fixed the same defect in the init --from-code emitter (src/brownfield/mod.rs blueprint_delta -> canonical '## ADDED Nodes/Edges'). refine additionally needs canonical '## REMOVED Nodes' and '## RENAMED Nodes' sections for its removals/renames, and bareword node names (the name slot is a bareword, multi-word names must collapse e.g. 'api gateway' -> 'ApiGateway'). AC: refine emits canonical delta the parser accepts; a refine->archive round-trip test asserts the blueprint reflects added/removed/renamed nodes; refine.rs unit tests updated from the old +/-/~ format.
close_reason: Closed

title: Scan finding: decision claims to close a spec Q-NN / supersede but registry+spec disagree
description: When a decision body asserts it closes a spec open-question (e.g. closes Q-03) or supersedes a spec example, but docs/registries/declared-items.md still lists that Q as open or docs/spec.md still shows the superseded text, that is silent prose drift cairn cannot currently catch. The managed skill cairn-ratify-convention-close-all-surfaces exists purely to prevent this by hand. Add a CAIRN_ advisory finding that cross-checks decision claim phrases (close(s) Q-NN, supersede(s)) against the declared-items registry status and spec presence, so ratify-a-convention-and-close-all-surfaces becomes a gate, not a procedure. Needs a new sequential error code.
close_reason: Implemented and merged: CA004 CAIRN_DECISION_CLAIM_UNRESOLVED in validate_decision_claims (src/artefacts/registry/validate), 3 tests passing, accepted dec.decision-claim-cross-check on main.

title: Add 'cairn decision new <slug>' artefact scaffold (deterministic frontmatter)
description: Authoring decisions/findings by hand has recurring frontmatter/ref/registry gotchas (evidenced by managed skills cairn-author-decision-and-finding-code and cairn-ratify-convention-close-all-surfaces). cairn already generates contracts (cairn contract) and has draft_* commands, so artefact generation precedent exists. Add 'cairn decision new <slug> [--node <id>] [--informed-by <ids>]' to scaffold meta/decisions/<slug>.md with correct frontmatter (id: dec.<slug>, nodes, status: proposed, date, informed_by) and standard sections (Context/Decision/Rationale/Consequences), validated so 'cairn scan --strict' passes on the stub. Consider generalizing to 'cairn artefact new <type>'. Converts a class of prose skills into one deterministic command.

Verified: 'cairn decision new <slug> [--node <id>] [--informed-by <id>]' scaffolds meta/decisions/dec.<slug>.md with correct frontmatter (id: dec.<slug>, nodes, status: proposed, date, and informed_by when --informed-by is passed) plus Context/Decision/Rationale/Consequences sections. The stub introduces zero decision-validation findings (scan-clean). Implemented in src/cli/commands/decision.rs, copy in [decision] of copy.toml, guarded by cli::commands::decision::tests::test_run_decision_new_writes_file_and_refuses_overwrite. The optional 'generalize to cairn artefact new' is not done and is out of scope for this bead's core acceptance.

title: cairn init emits no cairn-* agent skills: external agents have no on-ramp to the loop
description: Verified 2026-06-26. Greenfield init_project (src/cli/commands/project.rs:22-55) writes only cairn.blueprint, cairn.config.yaml, meta/contracts/.gitkeep, .cairn/state/.gitkeep, .cairn/AGENTS.md. The cairn-explore/propose/apply/archive skills live ONLY in cairn's own .claude/skills/ (git-tracked dotfiles); the binary embeds none of them (grep include_str!/include_bytes! = only UI assets, design-system css, copy.toml, agent_guide.md). The one emitted guide (agent_guide.md, 64 lines) does not even reference the explore/propose/apply/archive loop. So an external user who installs cairn gets a guide but zero on-ramp to the workflow that makes cairn useful. This is the distribution half of the 'agent uses cairn to max potential' plugin the maintainer wants.
acceptance_criteria: cairn init (or an explicit scaffold subcommand) emits/packages the cairn-* agent skills into a fresh repo, and agent_guide.md references the loop. Validated by running it on a clean external repo and confirming an agent can follow the loop.
close_reason: Delivered in PR #172 (squash d20b9a7): cairn init emits the curated cairn-* dev-loop skills into .claude/skills/ via include_str! single-source bundling + idempotent write loop; agent guide references the loop. Also corrected stale skill commands (cairn apply -> cairn-apply skill; manual mv -> cairn archive; openspec/ -> meta/changes/docs/registries) so the on-ramp is followable. Test test_init_project_emits_cairn_skills_and_loop_guide; all gates + E2E green.

title: Add strict lint+format gate for webui assets (src/ui_assets JS/CSS)
description: src/ui_assets/app.js (~70KB) and src/ui_assets/style.css (~50KB) have ZERO lint/format tooling. The repo gates Rust strictly (ci.yml + .pre-commit-config.yaml: clippy -D warnings, cargo fmt --check, cargo doc -D warnings, cargo test --locked) but nothing checks the hand-written webui JS/CSS. User wants linting strict for ALL languages, not just Rust.
design: Recommend biome (single static binary; lints+formats JS/CSS/JSON; no node_modules) over eslint+prettier+node — fits the repo's low-dependency, single-binary, strict ethos. Alternative: deno lint/deno fmt (also single binary). Wire it in: (1) .pre-commit-config.yaml — add a pre-commit format-check hook and a pre-push lint hook scoped to src/ui_assets (types or files: \.(js|css)$); (2) ci.yml — add a job step running the linter in --error/strict mode on main. title: Plan bd upgrade 1.0.4 -> 1.0.5+ (opt-in JSONL gotcha; bd github for cairn-y1m)
description: We are on bd 1.0.4 (2026-05-07). Latest released is 1.0.5 (2026-05-28); an unreleased 1.0.6 line adds heavy cross-clone Dolt merge-safety work. Tool is ~1 minor + unreleased behind. This bead is to PLAN the upgrade deliberately (not upgrade blindly) and harvest the synergies. Do NOT run bd upgrade across machines casually — see migration note below.
design:
SYNERGIES worth adopting:
1. bd github (native GitHub sync: pull/push/sync/status/repos; config github.token/owner/repo). Directly re-scopes cairn-y1m: bd may already do beads<->GitHub sync, so that spike becomes 'evaluate bd github' rather than build-custom. (Note added to cairn-y1m.)
2. 1.0.5 made JSONL auto-export OPT-IN and declared Dolt canonical / jsonl an optional export -- this formalizes the oracle ruling we adopted this session (Dolt canonical, jsonl passive). Aligns with cairn-2z9 (beads-as-task-layer): a native loader reading issues.jsonl REQUIRES export.auto=true.
3. Custom issue types (types.custom, server-validated in 1.0.5): we use a 'spike' LABEL today; could become a real custom type.
4. bd create --defer <date> -> deferred status: cleaner than leaving P3 spikes 'open' cluttering bd ready.
5. Ergonomics: bd close per-id reasons + idempotent re-close (hit this session), bd list --skip-labels, bd show count-only JSON.

GOTCHA (near-term, real for us): our config (.beads/config.yaml) sets NO export.auto / export.git-add. On 1.0.4 auto-export defaults ON; on 1.0.5+ it is OPT-IN. This repo has NO refs/dolt/* on origin -- beads sync here is the committed .beads/issues.jsonl, NOT Dolt remote. So if we upgrade and forget to set export.auto=true + export.git-add=true, the jsonl stops auto-refreshing/staging and our git-based bead sync silently breaks. MUST set those explicitly as part of the upgrade (or fully embrace Dolt-canonical and stop committing jsonl).

LATENT RISK (low for cairn now, real elsewhere): 1.0.4->1.0.5+ crosses FK/cascade migrations (0040-0042) and the unreleased dependencies-PK reshape (0050). Changelog: independently-migrated clones sharing a Dolt remote that cross these boundaries un-synced become PERMANENTLY un-mergeable (Dolt hard-refuses). cairn has no Dolt remote so this is dormant HERE, but any repo that bd dolt pushes needs the coordinated dance: push+pull every clone BEFORE upgrade, one designated migrator (BD_ALLOW_REMOTE_MIGRATE=1) migrates+pushes, others upgrade then pull. 1.0.5 forward-schema-skew guard hard-fails older bd against a newer-migrated DB.
acceptance_criteria: Produce a deterministic upgrade plan: (1) bump bd 1.0.4 -> latest; (2) BEFORE/AFTER set export.auto=true + export.git-add=true (or decide to drop jsonl-in-git and adopt Dolt-canonical) so bead sync does not break; (3) run bd doctor post-upgrade (migration-content-skew check); (4) for any Dolt-remote repos, follow the coordinated single-migrator procedure; (5) evaluate bd github sync against cairn-y1m's needs and update/close that spike accordingly; (6) decide whether to adopt custom 'spike' type and --defer for P3 spikes. No blind upgrade.
notes: Dolt status clarification: we ALREADY use Dolt — bd runs a local Dolt server (.beads/dolt, PID-backed) as its storage engine; nothing to 'implement'. What we do NOT use is Dolt REMOTE sync (confirmed: no refs/dolt/* on origin); cross-machine interchange is the git-committed issues.jsonl. The 1.0.6 (Unreleased) line is almost entirely cross-clone Dolt-REMOTE merge-safety: deterministic dependency/history PKs (migrations 0037/0050), per-migration content-hash + 'bd doctor' skew check, remote-migrate gate (BD_ALLOW_REMOTE_MIGRATE=1), FK-cascade repair on pull, is_blocked recompute, PK-fork recovery playbook. ALL dormant while we stay jsonl-in-git. So the embedded decision: KEEP jsonl-in-git (simple, human-diffable, upsert-only projection, low-risk upgrade since migration-0050 hazard is dormant with no Dolt remote) vs ADOPT bd dolt push/pull (real 3-way DB merge + audit sync, but every clone must coordinate migrations — what 1.0.6 hardens). One 1.0.6 item useful regardless: JSONL-export-in-pre-commit-hook (#3121) = atomic code+issues commits.
close_reason: Delivered deterministic upgrade plan: meta/decisions/bd-upgrade-plan.md. Pinned export.auto/git-add in .beads/config.yaml (upgrade-safe). Decision: keep jsonl-in-git, defer bd 1.0.4->1.0.5 bump (bd github + --defer already in 1.0.4; no Dolt remote so migration risk dormant). Re-scoped cairn-y1m to evaluate existing bd github.

Traces are immutable records, edge queues are mutable triage workflows with different lifecycles, so they belong in separate modules. Keep all of dev's implementation quality: CC002 validation gate, atomic writes (PID+nanos), dedicated QueueError enum, typed TraceStage enum, exported version constants. Add EdgeProvenance struct from experiment (links edges back to trace phases). Mechanical refactor: move files, update ~12 import paths across cli/hooks/query_api/tests, update cairn.blueprint. Zero logic changes needed. Reference: cairn-openspecdev src/suggested_edges/ and src/provenance/ on experiment/openspec-swarm branch.
close_reason: Delivered by merged PRs #110, #119, #111, #117, #118 respectively

title: Brownfield: add discovery/init/refine layer from experiment branch
description: Borrow the experiment/openspec-swarm branch's brownfield discovery pipeline as a new layer beneath the existing onboard command. The experiment's approach (discovery.rs, init.rs, refine.rs) provides cold-start filesystem scanning that works without an existing blueprint, complementing dev's heuristics/onboard which require a computed graph. The natural pipeline is: init --from-code (day 0 bootstrap) -> onboard (orphan classification with coupling scores) -> refine (periodic re-discovery). Implementation: port discovery/init/refine from experiment, wire cairn init --from-code and cairn refine CLI commands, keep dev's coupling-score heuristics as a refinement pass on discovered candidates. Reference: cairn-openspecdev src/brownfield/ on experiment/openspec-swarm branch.
close_reason: Delivered by merged PRs #110, #119, #111, #117, #118 respectively

title: Design+implement revisit-trigger relevance tension (spec:634)
description: spec.md:634 lists 'ADR revisit_triggers appear relevant based on recent changes' as a rationale tension. revisit_triggers is parsed (src/artefacts/registry/mod.rs:96), stored (types.rs:139), and rendered in the webui (app.js:1195), but no finding evaluates relevance. Unlike research-orphan, this needs a design decision for what 'relevant based on recent changes' means (change-correlation semantics), so it is a natural input to cairn-iy2 (the ghost-rule primitive). Surfaced by the cairn-mqe spec audit (meta/research/res.spec-designed-audit.md, gap 2).
acceptance_criteria: Either a decision artefact defining the relevance heuristic + an emitted advisory finding, or an explicit decision to keep it Declared pending cairn-iy2. No silent prose-only state.
notes: Gated on cairn-iy2: the relevance heuristic ('relevant based on recent changes') is part of the ghost-rule mechanism decision (dec.ghost-rule-tracking, proposed). Deferred until iy2 is ratified so this is not re-derived as a blocked seed each loop. Related to cairn-iy2.

title: Implement research-orphan tension (spec:632): warn on research not cited by any decision
description: spec.md:632 lists 'Research not linked from any decision' as a rationale tension, but it is unimplemented. validate_sources (src/artefacts/registry/validate/mod.rs:212) flags an uncited source (CAIRN_SOURCE_ORPHAN); there is no parallel check that a research artefact is cited by some decision. Data is already available: research_ids vs each decision.informed_by. Add a warning (e.g. CAIRN_RESEARCH_ORPHAN) for a research id absent from every decision.informed_by. Advisory only per spec. Surfaced by the cairn-mqe spec audit (meta/research/res.spec-designed-audit.md, gap 1).
acceptance_criteria: A research artefact cited by no decision emits a warning-severity CAIRN_RESEARCH_ORPHAN; a cited research artefact does not; behaviour-level test added; new code registered in docs/registries/error-codes.md.
close_reason: Implemented CAIRN_RESEARCH_ORPHAN (CA005) at info-level per spec:441 (PR #187). Surfaces research not linked from any decision; cairn's 2 exploratory orphans now show as info-level advisory, CI green.

title: Extract shared bead->JSON serializer across CLI, webui, and context
description: Bead-to-JSON formatting is duplicated in 3 places: src/ui/api.rs bead_json (webui inspector), src/query_api/handlers/project.rs backlog_item_json (context), and src/cli/render/project.rs render_backlog (cairn-aq5). If the bead JSON contract changes, the surfaces drift. Extract a single serializer (e.g. a method on backlog::BacklogItem in src/state/backlog.rs) and route all 3 call sites through it. Raised by CodeRabbit on PR #178 (non-blocking nitpick).
close_reason: Implemented BacklogItem::to_json single serializer (PR #186); all summary surfaces route through it; drift (missing node field) resolved.

description: Follow-up to cairn-qgn (consolidation+determinism-first done 2026-06-26). MAINTAINER-GATED product decision. Today cairn init's install pack single-sources the repo's own clean .claude/skills (cairn-dev/explore/propose/apply/archive) via include_str! (PR #172, cairn-48s). cairn-qgn classified the 7 GENERAL-capability managed skills as publish candidates: cairn-first-state-investigation, cairn-pr-landing, cairn-capture-brainstorm-as-artefact-chain, cairn-author-decision-and-finding-code, cairn-provenance-coverage, cairn-ratify-convention-close-all-surfaces, cairn-verify-shipped-before-next-steps. Scope: (1) decide WHICH (if any) to promote into the shipped install pack vs keep as personal tribal knowledge; (2) move chosen skills into the repo's .claude/skills/ and wire include_str! in src/cli/commands/project.rs (single-source); (3) per cairn-qgn Part 4, harden survivors with skill-creator evals (with-skill vs baseline + description optimization) BEFORE publishing, so the shipped surface meets a quality bar. Eval against the shipped pack showed the only non-overlapping lift was the provenance path; folded that into cairn-explore rather than ship overlapping skills. Other 6 managed skills assessed keep-personal (redundant/niche/contradicting). Promotion policy now: judge marginal lift over the current pack, prefer merge over new skill.

title: changes::archive resolves blueprint_path inconsistently (load_project cwd-relative vs apply_archive root-relative)
description: src/changes/mod.rs archive() calls scanner::load_project(root, blueprint_path) which does blueprint::parse_file(blueprint_path) (resolved against CWD), but src/changes/apply/mod.rs apply_archive() reads root.join(blueprint_path). Via the CLI this is masked because cwd==root and parsed.file is relative. A programmatic caller passing a root-relative blueprint_path from a different cwd validates against the wrong blueprint (discovered while testing cairn-h8o; tests must pass an absolute root.join path). Fix: make load_project join root, or document blueprint_path must be absolute/root-resolved. Low priority: no CLI impact.
close_reason: Closed

title: Reconcile duplicate VerificationState enum (dead public module vs private accept.rs copy)
description: Verified 2026-06-26. src/verification.rs defines a public 5-state VerificationState (pub mod at lib.rs:58) referenced by NO consumer (rg shows zero imports outside the module itself) = dead code. The live path is a PRIVATE duplicate enum in src/cli/accept.rs:173 exercising 3 states (Passed/Failed/Blocked) with Draft/Planned #[allow(dead_code)]. This is precisely the kind of code/intent drift cairn sells protection against, sitting in cairn's own source.
acceptance_criteria: One VerificationState (accept.rs uses crate::verification::VerificationState; private duplicate deleted), no #[allow(dead_code)] on its variants, cargo build + clippy + test green.
close_reason: Already reconciled on main (f16dae8). Verified: exactly one VerificationState enum (src/verification.rs); src/cli/accept.rs uses 'use crate::verification::VerificationState' (line 6) with no private duplicate enum and no #[allow(dead_code)] on its variants; the module is consumed so it is not dead. Gates green (test_verification_state_serde_roundtrip + accept.rs state_str tests pass). Acceptance criteria fully met.

title: Preserve blueprint comments when applying a non-empty delta on archive
description: apply_blueprint_delta -> serialize_ast regenerates cairn.blueprint from the AST, which discards comments and blank lines. PR #157 fixed the empty-delta case (skip the write). For NON-empty deltas (real node/edge changes), archiving still strips comments. A full fix needs the lexer/parser to capture comment trivia and the serializer to re-emit it (round-trip fidelity). Larger feature; needs a trivia-model design decision before implementing. Low urgency: only triggers on structural-delta archives.
close_reason: Source-preserving delta splice preserves comments/blank lines on non-empty-delta archive (PR)

title: Remove dead --json branches in CLI order/contract arms
description: The order and contract command arms in src/cli/mod.rs carry inline 'if parsed.json' branches that are unreachable: run_project_command intercepts --json at line 304 (uses_shared_json) and routes to query_api before render_loaded_project_command runs. The dead contract branch even omits the 'contracts' field that query_api emits. Remove the dead branches so query_api is the single JSON source of truth.
acceptance_criteria: order/contract arms emit only human output; cairn order --json and cairn contract <n> --json byte-identical (query_api-served); test pins contract --json includes 'contracts' field; all gates green
close_reason: Merged in PR #153 (c0c7262): removed unreachable --json branches; query_api is single JSON source of truth; regression test added; all gates green.

title: Landing page design-token conformance (52 hardcoded hex)
description: docs/landing/index.html (1350 lines) hardcodes ~52 hex colours despite CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md requiring the landing surface to source colours from docs/design-system/tokens.css. The webui stylesheet was brought to conformance and gated in PR #145 (scripts/check-design-tokens.sh); the landing page is the remaining surface with the same drift. Note: some hex values may be intentional brand colours not yet in the token set, so this needs a maintainer call on token mapping vs new tokens, plus visual verification (the page uses IntersectionObserver reveal animations).
acceptance_criteria: Each hardcoded hex in docs/landing/index.html is either replaced by a var(--token) (adding tokens to docs/design-system/tokens.css where a brand colour has no token, updating the live reference + README per the design-system update rule) or explicitly justified; docs/landing/index.html then passes scripts/check-design-tokens.sh (parameterised via CAIRN_DESIGN_TOKENS_TARGET); the gate is extended to cover the landing target; page renders unchanged (visual check).
notes:
Discovered during PR #145 (cairn-loop 2026-06-23). The gate built there already supports an arbitrary target via CAIRN_DESIGN_TOKENS_TARGET, so extending enforcement is a one-line wiring change once the page is conformant. Large/design-sensitive: keep it its own unit.
CORRECTION (cairn-loop 2026-06-23, after investigation): the '52 hardcoded hex' premise is INACCURATE. All 52 hex are inline CSS custom-property DEFINITIONS in two theme blocks (:root dark at line 26, [data-theme="light"] at line 101), not hardcoded value usages. grep confirms ZERO hex used as values (color:/background: etc.) outside token definitions: the page already uses var(--token) everywhere and is usage-conformant.

REAL ISSUE = the landing FORKS the design-system palette inline (violates docs/design-system/README.md do-not-fork rule + CLAUDE.md 'marketing via <link>'), and the fork has DRIFTED: dark theme 26/26 tokens match canonical exactly; light theme 21/26 drifted (small warm-tone deltas, e.g. --stone-5 #ffffff vs canonical #fcf8ed, --stone-0 #e8e2d3 vs #e8ddc4).

SCOPED FIX (ready to execute, needs maintainer GO because it changes the PUBLIC auto-deployed site): add <link rel=stylesheet href=../design-system/tokens.css> to <head> (Pages serves all of docs/, so the relative path resolves); delete the inlined COLOUR token defs from both theme blocks; KEEP page-specific non-colour tokens (--t-*, --s-*, --r-edge/stone/round, --ease/--fast/--med/--slow, --font-*, the rgba --*-wash). Do NOT link components.css (bespoke page, collision risk) or fonts.css (page already <link>s Google Fonts). Effect: default dark view pixel-identical; light view reconciles to canonical (sanctioned drift-correction). Then the page has zero hardcoded hex and CAN be added to scripts/check-design-tokens.sh. Requires before/after visual verification (dark parity, light coherence). Merge auto-deploys via pages.yml, so this is a maintainer-approved outward action.
close_reason: Landing sources colour from canonical design-system tokens.css; fork deleted; gate extended to cover the landing; PR #146 merged (e671126).

title: Gate dependency-edge drift (dec.graph-root-fingerprint)
description: Close the dependency-edge-drift gap ratified in meta/decisions/graph-root-fingerprint.md (dec.graph-root-fingerprint). Maintainer-gated: do NOT start until George greenlights. Today declared dependency edges are endpoint-validated only (src/map/build.rs validate_edges, CAIRN_INTEGRITY_INVALID_EDGE_ENDPOINT) and built in-memory each scan; BlueprintSnapshot (src/scanner/state.rs:65-70) records nodes only, and the change gate (src/scanner/checks.rs:48-67) iterates node add/remove/parent/kind only. So changing a node's declared dependency-edge set is a structural change with NO covering-decision gate, unlike a node kind/parent change. Work: (1) record each node's outbound edge set in BlueprintSnapshot (or a sibling snapshot keyed by node id, deterministically ordered) bumping the schema version; (2) extend check_blueprint_change_decisions to emit CAIRN_BLUEPRINT_CHANGE_NO_DECISION for a node whose declared edge set changed without a covering decision; (3) test the new finding fires/clears. DEFERRED (NOT this bead): the aggregate graph-root Merkle fingerprint and Cairn-Graph-Root commit trailer remain deferred per the decision (no consumer; DefaultHasher is toolchain-unstable for a committed value). Spawned by spike cairn-t59.
close_reason: Implemented in PR #142: dependency-edge drift gate. BlueprintSnapshot schema v2 records per-node outbound edges; check_blueprint_change_decisions emits CAIRN_BLUEPRINT_CHANGE_NO_DECISION on edge-set change without a covering decision; v1 snapshots migrate via migrate_v1_to_v2; edge checks gated on previous.version >= 2 to avoid spurious findings on first scan after upgrade.

title: Browser UI/UX iterative AI fix loop
description: Plan and implement an iterative testing loop for the cairn webui: use browser control (headless Chromium / Playwright or similar) to take screenshots, feed them to an AI vision model, receive fix instructions, apply them to the UI assets, and re-screenshot until the UI meets acceptance criteria. Loop: screenshot -> AI critique -> patch src/ui_assets/ -> reload -> repeat. Scope includes: tooling selection (Playwright vs puppeteer), prompt design for AI critique, integration point in the dev workflow (standalone script vs cairn command), and a repeatable acceptance spec (no regressions in layout/accessibility/design-token conformance).
notes:
BLOCKER (cairn-loop 2026-06-23): integration-point decision = standalone script (under scripts/). But the core browser->AI-critique->patch->reload loop is blocked on two maintainer prerequisites that cannot be guessed: (1) sanction to introduce a Node + Playwright/puppeteer toolchain into this package.json-less Rust repo (adds node_modules, CI cost); (2) an AI vision provider/model + API config (none exists; likely paid; conflicts with the repo's deterministic-gates convention). The deterministic acceptance-spec portion (design-token conformance / a11y static audit of src/ui_assets) is buildable without either and is the natural first standalone unit if a smaller scope is wanted. Released to open; not actively in progress.
DETERMINISTIC SUB-SCOPE LANDED (cairn-loop 2026-06-23, PR #145, merge 82932f9): scripts/check-design-tokens.sh now gates hardcoded hex/rem in src/ui_assets/style.css (wired into pre-commit + CI webui job + Makefile; covered by tests/check_design_tokens.rs; recorded in dec.webui-design-token-gate). Remaining cairn-y7p scope is ONLY the AI-vision loop (browser->AI-critique->patch->reload), still blocked on the two maintainer prerequisites (Node/Playwright toolchain sanction + an AI vision provider/API). The a11y static-audit half of the deterministic slice is still open if a smaller next unit is wanted.
DETERMINISTIC A11Y SLICE LANDED (cairn-loop 2026-06-23, PR #148, merge b38d8eb): scripts/check-a11y.sh now gates the hand-authored web surfaces (src/ui_assets/index.html, src/ui_assets/app.js, docs/landing/index.html) for WCAG 1.1.1 img-alt, 2.4.3 positive-tabindex, and (documents only) 3.1.1 lang / 2.4.2 title / 1.4.4 zoom-not-disabled. Wired into pre-commit + CI webui job + Makefile; covered by tests/check_a11y.rs; recorded in dec.webui-a11y-static-audit-gate. BOTH deterministic halves of cairn-y7p (design-token gate PR #145 + a11y gate PR #148) are now done. Remaining cairn-y7p scope is ONLY the AI-vision loop, still BLOCKED on two maintainer prerequisites that cannot be guessed: (1) sanction for a Node + Playwright/puppeteer toolchain in this package.json-less Rust repo; (2) an AI vision provider/model + API config (none exists; likely paid; conflicts with the deterministic-gates convention).
close_reason: Deterministic halves shipped (PR #145 design-token gate, PR #148 a11y gate). AI-vision loop declined by maintainer as out of scope for a deterministic, package.json-less Rust repo; recorded in dec.webui-ai-vision-loop-declined. Reopening requires a superseding decision sanctioning a Node/Playwright toolchain + a vision provider.

title: Spike: cairn warns when a tracked toolchain's linting isn't strict
description: User idea (marked '(maybe)'): cairn should emit a FINDING when a tracked project's linting is not configured strict — e.g. a Rust crate without clippy -D warnings / cargo fmt, or a JS/TS surface with no linter at all. Capture for ratification; do NOT build yet. Surfaced 2026-06-17 alongside the webui-lint gap (it would have flagged src/ui_assets being unlinted).
design: Scope-fit analysis is the deliverable. Cairn today reconciles the ARCHITECTURE map vs reality (interface-hash drift, orphans, provenance). 'Is linting strict' is a TOOLCHAIN-CONFIG convention check — one layer beyond architecture, closer to project-health. It IS existence/linkage-shaped (does a strict-lint config exist for each detected language?), which mirrors cairn's existing existence checks (contracts present spec.md:318, todo coverage spec.md:808, and the cairn-a8z test-coverage gate) — so arguably in-bounds as an ADVISORY finding, NOT a blocker. Design if adopted: new finding code (e.g. CAIRN_LINT_NOT_STRICT), advisory/warning default with opt-in strict gate (same knob pattern as spec.md:808/635); per-language detection that only inspects CONFIG (Rust: clippy -D warnings + fmt in CI/.pre-commit/Makefile; JS/TS: eslint/biome/deno config present; etc.), and NEVER invokes a linter. Risk to weigh: cairn drifting into a generic linter-of-linters, against the 'map of what exists / fence not proof-system' non-goals (spec.md:149/699/837). Sibling of cairn-a8z (both: cairn checking verification-surface health by existence/linkage, never running tools).
acceptance_criteria: Spike output = a ratified decision/ADR (adopt or reject), NOT implementation. If adopt: define the finding code + default severity (advisory, strict opt-in), the per-language config-detection rules (config existence/strictness only), and the explicit NON-GOAL recorded (cairn never runs linters — only checks one is configured strict). Cross-reference cairn-a8z.
close_reason: Ratified in meta/decisions/toolchain-lint-strictness.md via PR #143.

title: Spike: graph-root fingerprint bound to git commits (git-native knowledge graph, NOT a Dolt-style store)
description: Design question raised by user: 'how dolt is git for sql, should cairn consider being git for knowledge graph?' Verdict reached this session: NO to a versioned graph STORE (the Dolt-analogue); YES to a content-addressed fingerprint OF the reconciled graph, bound to git commits. This spike captures that fork to ratify or reject as a decision/ADR.
design:
WHY NOT a versioned graph DB: Dolt exists because SQL had no native version control (mutable-in-place). Cairn's source of truth is ALREADY git-native -- blueprint+artifacts are markdown blobs, code is source files, all in git's content-addressed object store; git already gives history/branch/merge/diff for cairn's authored inputs. The graph is DERIVED/reconciled, not authored -- you version inputs and recompute, you don't version a projection. A second canonical store = the two-source-of-truth drift trap cairn exists to kill (same lesson as jsonl-vs-Dolt this session). So a Dolt-style graph store is self-contradictory + premature-abstraction.

WHAT IS RIGHT (mostly already built): cairn already fingerprints reality -- InterfaceFingerprint (deterministic content hash of sorted public symbols, per target/node; src/reconcile/fingerprint.rs) and NodeFingerprint/BlueprintSnapshot (versioned structural-shape hash; src/scanner/state.rs). MISSING piece = aggregation: a graph-ROOT hash (Merkle-style root over {all node fingerprints + edges + interface hashes}, deterministic ordering) summarizing the whole reconciled map in one hash, BOUND to the git commit (commit trailer 'Cairn-Graph-Root: <hash>', git note, or tracked .cairn/state/root). Payoffs: arch-changed-between-A-and-B = compare two roots (semantic, O(1), not markdown text-diff); commit-vs-declared-graph consistency = recompute+compare (existing drift gate lifted from per-contract to whole-graph); provenance = decision->contract->code chained into one verifiable per-commit fingerprint (the two-chain integrity, anchored in git's hash chain). Framing: Dolt = git FOR sql (new store); cairn = git-NATIVE knowledge graph (compose WITH git, add a derived Merkle root). Ties to existing integrity work in src/map/integrity.rs.
acceptance_criteria: This is a SPIKE -> output is a ratified decision/ADR + design, NOT implementation. Produce: (1) the graph-root hash algorithm (Merkle over node fingerprints + edges + interface hashes; deterministic node/edge ordering; reuse InterfaceFingerprint); (2) the commit-binding mechanism choice (trailer vs git note vs tracked .cairn/state/root) with tradeoffs (diff noise, merge behavior, tamper-evidence); (3) how it composes with the existing drift/scan gate and BlueprintSnapshot change-detection without a second source of truth; (4) explicit NON-GOAL recorded: no versioned graph DB, no Dolt-style store, no separate canonical graph store. Decide adopt vs defer; if adopt, file implementation beads.
close_reason: Spike resolved: ratified dec.graph-root-fingerprint (meta/decisions/graph-root-fingerprint.md). Verdict: reject versioned graph store; adopt derived Merkle-style graph-root fingerprint bound to a git commit via Cairn-Graph-Root trailer; recompute-and-compare stays the gate (no second source of truth). Implementation deferred to maintainer-gated cairn-9v1.

title: Spike: beads as cairn's first-class task layer (per-node todos)
description: Close the gap between cairn's two disconnected task worlds: cairn HAS a first-class Todo artefact type (markdown in meta/todos/, surfaced per-node in the webui Todos panel) but this repo doesn't use it; task tracking is done in beads (bd), which are NOT in the cairn graph and invisible to the webui. Proposal: make node-linked BEADS the backing store for cairn's task layer, so a node's related tasks (with bd workflow state) appear in the webui per-node inspector. Parent unification behind cairn-y1m (beads<->GitHub label sync) and cairn-a8z (TDD/coverage gate, whose bead workflow-state would ride here). KEY: tasks remain navigation/context (declared status), NOT enforcement -- bead status is a claim cairn surfaces, not a reconciled fact (unchanged from the declared-vs-verified distinction).
design:
Pipeline finding: the consuming chain is storage-agnostic. load_todos (src/artefacts/registry/mod.rs:44) scans markdown -> Todo struct -> todo_json (query_api/serialise.rs:31) -> todos_response_json (handlers/artefacts.rs:8) -> UI server (src/ui/server.rs:188 'todos') -> webui Todos panel (app.js:1171, fetchNodeArtefacts(id,'todos') app.js:1718). Everything downstream consumes Vec<Todo>; only the LOADER reads markdown. So beads-as-task-layer changes only where todos load from.

TWO IMPLEMENTATION SHAPES:
1. Export bridge (low risk, zero kernel change): a transform turns node-tagged beads into meta/todos/<node>/<id>.md (node:/status: frontmatter) via bd export; blueprint gets todos pointers; cairn ingests as normal markdown todos. Pros: reuses entire pipeline, preserves 'artefacts are markdown' invariant (spec.md:11), git-native. Cons: generated files, a sync step, staleness window, two on-disk representations.
2. Native beads loader (integrated): add a beads artefact-source to load_todos that reads .beads/issues.jsonl, filters by a cairn-node:<id> label, maps bead->Todo. Downstream unchanged. Pros: single source of truth (beads), live, no generated markdown, gains bd workflow machinery (set-state/deps/formulas) as the task layer. Cons: introduces an external Dolt-backed/jsonl-projected source into the otherwise-markdown artefact model (bends spec.md:11); must handle jsonl staleness vs canonical Dolt.

MECHANISM DETAILS:
- Node link: a cairn-node:<id> bead label (colon labels are idiomatic in bd, cf. set-state dimension:value). Optional satisfies via metadata mirroring the Todo satisfies: field.
- Status mapping is clean: cairn TodoStatus = open|in_progress|done|blocked (spec.md:329) aligns with bd statuses.
- Integrity rule (mirror spec.md:339): every task-bead's cairn-node label must resolve to a real node; orphans = warnings.
- Source-of-truth: beads (Dolt) canonical; .beads/issues.jsonl is the git-tracked reality layer cairn reads. MUST NOT also keep markdown todos -- dual representation is the divergence trap (the second-source-of-truth risk also flagged in cairn-y1m).
acceptance_criteria: Produce a deterministic ruling (decision artefact/design.md) resolving: (1) shape 1 (export bridge) vs shape 2 (native beads loader) and why; (2) whether a non-markdown artefact source is acceptable in cairn's model, or todos stay markdown-defined (ruling on spec.md:11 invariant); (3) the node-link convention (label key, e.g. cairn-node:<id>) + status mapping table + satisfies carry; (4) integrity rule for orphan task-beads; (5) explicit confirmation tasks remain navigation/context not enforcement (declared-not-verified preserved); (6) how this relates to cairn-y1m (GitHub mirror) and cairn-a8z (TDD state). No implementation in this spike.
close_reason: Spike resolved by dec.beads-task-layer (PR #149, merge ae38af9): surface node-linked beads as a read-only derived view over backlog.rs, not Todo artefacts and not an export bridge; spec.md:11 untouched. Implementation is a follow-up.

title: Spike: state-dependent test-coverage integrity gate
description: Cairn should FLAG (and optionally GATE) modules whose code exists but has no linked tests. Cairn never runs tests (non-goal spec.md:837); it only checks existence/linkage of the verification surface declared in the contract's ## Tests section. Recurring user wish; refined over a design discussion 2026-06-17. Related to cairn-y1m (beads-as-workflow) and Phase 5 edge validation.
design:
Keyed to node reconciliation state (spec.md:611-613), NOT beads workflow state, because cairn only observes the reality layer:
- ghost (declared, no code yet / planned) -> test NOT required, no divergence.
- synced (code exists) -> test linkage expected; missing = divergence.
This mirrors the existing contract rule exactly: missing contract is a warning EXCEPT at ghost->synced transition where it becomes required (spec.md:318). Elegant property: cairn infers 'tests expected now' from 'code exists now' -- no new workflow-state input needed. In correct TDD the test edge already exists when code lands, so the gate never fires; skip the test and it fires immediately. Cairn = backstop; beads = guide.

DETECTION = an edge-validation problem (Phase 5, spec.md:635/770): does a test->module edge exist in the reality layer? OPEN QUESTION -- pick mechanism:
(a) path/convention: reconciler finds sibling test files / inline #[cfg(test)] / tests/ mirror. Zero authoring, language-specific heuristics.
(b) import-edge via LSP: a test file references the module's public symbols. Most accurate, needs Phase 5 LSP/tree-sitter.
(c) explicit ## Tests declaration: contract names expected test paths/IDs; reconciler verifies they exist and are non-empty. Most cairn-idiomatic (contract = the norm), human-authored.
Recommend hybrid: contract ## Tests declares intent (norm); reconciler verifies reality realizes it (a/b). Missing realization = divergence, exactly the two-chain contract->code conformance pattern.

FLAG vs GATE = strict/loose per-project config knob (user's 'linter strict mode' analogy). Precedent: todo coverage loose-by-default/opt-in-strict (spec.md:808); edge divergence advisory-by-default (spec.md:635). Loose: divergence = rationale tension (warning). Strict: blocks commit like an interface-hash contradiction (spec.md:320).

BOUNDARY: cairn asserts tests EXIST and LINK, never that they PASS or are MEANINGFUL (fence-not-proof-system, spec.md:699; no test-runner binding, spec.md:837). No new artefact type -- reuses contract ## Tests section + a new finding type + config severity.
acceptance_criteria: Produce a deterministic ruling (decision artefact or design.md) that resolves: (1) which detection mechanism (a/b/c/hybrid) and why; (2) exact state trigger (confirm ghost->synced parity with contract rule); (3) config key + default severity (loose) and strict semantics; (4) finding type name + error code; (5) confirm boundary (existence/linkage only). No implementation in this spike -- output is the spec/decision that a follow-up feature phase implements.
close_reason: Resolved by cairn-87n (feat(map): add CAIRN_TEST_COVERAGE_MISSING gate); spike verdict + implementation both shipped.

title: Investigate syncing bead status with GitHub issues via labels
description:
Spike (do NOT implement yet): evaluate whether and how cairn should sync beads issue state with GitHub issues using labels/tags.

Inspiration: ForgeDock (https://github.com/RapierCraftStudios/ForgeDock) uses GitHub as the agent memory/coordination layer. Relevant patterns:
- Workflow-state LABELS on GitHub issues: workflow:investigating, workflow:building, workflow:in-review, workflow:merged. The pipeline resumes from whatever state GitHub reports (restart-safe).
- Structured FORGE: annotations (HTML comments) written to issues/PRs as durable cross-session context; gh CLI is the query interface.

Questions to answer:
1. SHOULD we? Beads already has its own canonical store (local Dolt DB, refs/dolt/data for cross-machine sync). GitHub-issue sync is a SECOND source of truth - assess conflict/divergence risk vs benefit. Does it duplicate what dolt push/pull already gives us?
2. COULD we? Map bead status (open/in_progress/closed) and priority to a label scheme (e.g. bead:open, bead:in-progress, bead:p0). One-way (bead->GH) or bidirectional? Bidirectional reintroduces the merge-conflict class we just hit with issues.jsonl.
3. Mechanism: gh CLI from a cairn hook? A bd plugin? A GitHub Action that reads .beads/issues.jsonl on push and reconciles labels? Where does the mapping live?
4. Identity: how to link a bead id (cairn-xxx) to a GH issue number - store gh issue number in bead metadata, or a label like bead-id:cairn-xxx?
5. Scope boundary vs the existing OMP/cairn task-sync work (cairn-d7s diagnostics server) - is this the same surface or distinct?

Deliverable of the spike: a recommendation (do / do-not / defer) with a concrete design sketch if 'do', plus identified risks. No code in this bead.
notes:
bd already ships native GitHub sync: 'bd github' (pull/push/sync/status/repos), config via github.token/owner/repo. Re-scope this spike to FIRST evaluate 'bd github sync' (does it cover workflow-state labels / status mirroring?) vs building a custom ForgeDock-style label layer. May reduce this to config + a thin policy rather than new code. See cairn upgrade bead.
RE-SCOPE (dec.bd-upgrade-plan, 2026-06-23): 'bd github' (pull/push/sync) is ALREADY present in bd 1.0.4 — no upgrade needed to get native bead<->GitHub sync. This spike is now: (a) MAINTAINER do/don't on accepting GitHub issues as a second source of truth (divergence risk vs jsonl-in-git + Dolt-local canonical); (b) if do, one-way bead->GH vs bidirectional, and the bead-id<->issue-number identity mapping. Custom-build of a ForgeDock-style label layer is NOT needed; evaluate 'bd github' config (github.token/owner/repo) + a thin policy first.
close_reason: Spike resolved by dec.bead-github-sync (recommendation: defer / do-not adopt GitHub issues as a second source of truth). Ratified via PR #144.
